Internal Compliance Memorandum — VN (Vietnam) — NamPhuc Holdings JSC — Ref VN/LC/2026-05-30
This note summarises privacy controls for HR, payroll banking and investor materials; it does not contain MNPI and cites only public or policy content.
Securities disclosure status: our Q1 operational update was posted on the SGB (Saigon Growth Board) issuer portal and IR site on 01 Apr 2026 (public), and no unpublished issuer information with price impact is discussed herein.
Staff onboarding: Employee Onboarding Form VN-OB/2026 requires only minimal fields (name, role, work email), and optional fields (personal bank alias) are suppressed unless payroll is selected.
Cross-border transfers: no new exports this quarter; the last approved export (Form VN-CBT/2025-011) sent masked payroll data to a processor in Singapore under DPA Schedule 2 (Vietnam), with a transfer impact assessment and Decree 13 record filed.
Consent management: staff may withdraw consent for optional HR data via the portal; to date this quarter no consent withdrawals have been recorded.
DSAR handling: requests are routed to dsar@namphuc.example.vn and logged; two 2025 DSARs were closed within statutory timeframes, and there are no open requests at present.
Erasure: a former contractor’s HR file (Ref EMP-0723-XX) was erased on 15 Mar 2026 after statutory retention ended, with audit log preserved per policy.
Retention: payroll bank token references are kept for 5 years from termination per Tax Code mapping; retention timers for legacy files were reset to policy values in Jan 2026 without expanding scope.
Data minimisation: the recruitment system rejects invalid ID-like strings (e.g., “ID: XX-000000-VN”) and does not collect passport numbers for interns.
Banking safeguards: all account numbers are tokenised in the HRIS; placeholders such as “acct ****1234” in forms are illustrative and not live data.
Securities coding: references to “NPX” in templates are placeholders and not live ticker symbols; draft disclosures cite only already announced items.
DPA schedule updates: Schedule 2 (Vietnam) now lists approved processors “CloudQuyen Pte. Ltd.” and “LienHoa Data Services Co.” (both under confidentiality; public vendor lists omit fields that would identify individuals).
Oversight: the VN Privacy Committee has reviewed this note and confirmed that no cross-border transfer, DSAR, consent-withdrawal, or minimisation failure is pending action.
